text.title: Chroma — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step: client setup → collection with metric → upsert with string IDs → query → backup.

section.title: Step 1 — Persistent client
section.p: `client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_db")`. Reject in-memory `Client()` outside of unit tests. Apply r5.

section.title: Step 2 — get_or_create_collection with metric
section.p: `coll = client.get_or_create_collection(name="documents", metadata={"hnsw:space": "cosine"})`. Document the chosen metric in code comments. Apply r1, r3.

section.title: Step 3 — Upsert with string IDs
section.p: `coll.upsert(ids=[str(i) for i in ...], embeddings=[...], metadatas=[...])`. Use upsert, not add. Apply r2.

section.title: Step 4 — Query with metric-aware top_k
section.p: `coll.query(query_embeddings=[q], n_results=top_k, where={...})`. Pass payload filters via `where` clause; do not post-filter in Python.

section.title: Step 5 — Backup the persistence dir
section.p: Snapshot the `./chroma_db` directory before any schema change. Restore is a copy-back; there is no online migration.
